title: aster undulatus var. diversifolius
intro: Aster undulatus var. diversifolius, also known as the White Wood Aster, is a variety of flowering plant in the daisy family native to the southeastern United States. This plant is known for its medicinal properties, particularly its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which have been traditionally used to treat wounds, skin irritations, and respiratory issues. The health benefits of the White Wood Aster include its ability to reduce inflammation, promote wound healing, and alleviate symptoms of colds and flu. The herbal preparations of this plant include infusions, tinctures, and salves, which can be used topically or taken internally to harness its medicinal properties.
